reasoning: The FRED statistical trend and seasonal combined estimate gives a 59% probability of the high yield index exceeding its latest benchmark in early April. The yield jumped rapidly by ~30 bps in late March 2025 amid growing recession fears, and US high yield credit spreads—which had been running at historically tight levels (~322 bps)—show clear signs of widening. While some short-term mean reversion typically follows such swift moves, the structural widening of spreads supports higher yields over the immediate front-end horizon. Longer-term, potential Fed rate cuts could reduce the underlying Treasury rates, partially or fully offsetting the spread widening. Following instructions, the probabilities linearly regress towards 0.50 as the resolution horizon extends to 2026, reflecting increasing uncertainty over evolving macroeconomic conditions.
